entities.name: GULLY EROSION
entities.description: A widespread problem of soil erosion characterized by the formation of gullies, particularly in semi-arid regions.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGION
entities.description: Geographic areas characterized by low rainfall, denuded landscapes, and flash floods.

entities.name: DENUDED LANDSCAPE
entities.description: Land that has been stripped of its vegetation cover.

entities.name: FLASH FLOODS
entities.description: Rapid flooding of low-lying areas, contributing to gully erosion.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where approximately 4 million hectares of land are affected by severe gully erosion.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where approximately 29 million hectares of land are affected by severe gully erosion.

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The general process of soil displacement, of which gully erosion is a more difficult and expensive type to control.

entities.name: GULLY STABILIZATION
entities.description: The objective of achieving stability in gullies to prevent further erosion.

entities.name: GULLY FORMATION
entities.description: The process and causes leading to the creation of gullies.

entities.name: WATERSHED DEVELOPMENT AND MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The overall approach to managing water and land resources within a watershed, including gully control.

entities.name: GULLY CONTROL STRUCTURES
entities.description: Physical interventions designed to stabilize gullies and manage runoff.

entities.name: LOOSE ROCK DAM
entities.description: A type of gully control structure constructed from loose rocks.

entities.name: DOUBLE-ROW POST-BRUSH DAM
entities.description: A gully control structure utilizing two rows of posts and brush material.

entities.name: DOUBLE-ROW POST-STONE DAM
entities.description: A gully control structure utilizing two rows of posts and stones.

entities.name: SINGLE-ROW POST-STONE DAM
entities.description: A gully control structure utilizing a single row of posts and stones.

entities.name: STONE WALL DAM
entities.description: A gully control structure consisting of a wall made of stones.

entities.name: MASONRY CHECK-DAM
entities.description: A gully control structure built using masonry materials.

entities.name: EARTHEN CHECK-DAM
entities.description: A gully control structure constructed from soil/earth.

entities.name: GABION STRUCTURES
entities.description: Gully control structures made of wire mesh baskets filled with rocks.

entities.name: RUNOFF VOLUME
entities.description: The total amount of water runoff measured at the watershed scale, used for designing control structures.

entities.name: PEAK RUNOFF RATE
entities.description: The maximum rate of runoff measured at the watershed scale, used for designing control structures.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Center for Semi-Arid Tropics, which implemented on-farm watershed projects.
